# One-off — CLARA MAASS MEDICAL CENTER (NJ)

```
**To:** President/CEO, Clara Maass Medical Center
**CC:** Chief Operating Officer, RWJBarnabas Health
**Subject:** N.J.S.A. 26:2H-5.17 Compliance: Detect & Response Infrastructure

New Jersey's Violence Prevention in Health Care Facilities Act requires documented detection and alert systems across ED and behavioral units. Clara Maass—472 beds, high uncompensated care—faces liability risk without real-time gunshot and threat localization.

Firefly's Ember software + Lattice hardware (panic buttons, acoustic detection, private-network gateway) closes the gap: Detect incidents in <1 second, Locate staff precisely, Alert responders, enable Respond protocols—all without IT dependency.

I'd like to discuss how other NJ safety-net hospitals are meeting 26:2H-5.17 requirements. Could we find 15 minutes this month?

— Ryan

---
**Why this opening:** New Jersey's Violence Prevention mandate is in force and Clara Maass's ED + behavioral unit are in regulatory scope; the facility's safety-net status and 16-facility system scale make compliance infrastructure a board-level concern.
```
